Title: Malaysia's hydrography role crucial in keeping Strait Of Malacca safe and open
Article:
PORT KLANG: Malaysia's hydrography capabilities play a critical role in ensuring the Strait of Malacca remains safe and open, says National Hydrography Centre.
Director-general Rear Admiral Dr Najhan Md Said said the strait was a vital artery for the global economy, handling traffic volumes more than three times that of the Strait of Hormuz.
"We often hear about key routes such as the Suez Canal and Panama Canal, and now the Strait of Hormuz, but the Strait of Malacca is the busiest in terms of traffic density," he said at a press conference in conjunction with World Hydrography Day 2026 at the National Hydrography Centre.
He said safeguarding the strait could not rest on a single agency, but required coordinated efforts among all maritime safety stakeholders.
"The safety of the Strait of Malacca is the lifeline of the world economy. However, it cannot be the responsibility of one agency alone, including the National Hydrography Centre," he added.
Najhan said the centre's role extended beyond defence, as it serves as Malaysia's national hydrographic authority and represents the country at the International Hydrographic Organisation in Monaco.
He said hydrography involved the collection and mapping of maritime data such as water depths, much of which goes unnoticed by the public.
"Our work is behind the scenes. We go out to sea to collect data, conduct mapping and produce charts," he added.
Earlier in his speech, Najhan said ocean data had become a strategic asset supporting national security, economic growth, environmental sustainability and public welfare.
He said the growing importance of maritime data had placed hydrography at the centre of efforts to ensure safe navigation and strengthen maritime security.
"Data integration allows decisions to be made faster, more accurately and effectively," he added.
Najhan said that greater cooperation in sharing knowledge, responsibilities and data was essential to build a safer and more integrated marine ecosystem.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar pays condolence visit to Nancy Shukri
Article:
KUCHING: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today paid a condolence visit to Women, Family and Community Development Minister Datuk Seri Nancy Shukri following the passing of her husband, Datuk Kamil Misuari.
Anwar arrived at Nancy's residence at No. 6, Taman Serira, Lorong Menggris 2A, Petra Jaya, at 5.45pm after attending an event in Bintulu.
Accompanied by Sarawak Deputy Premier Datuk Amar Douglas Uggah Embas, he spent about 20 minutes at the residence before leaving for the Borneo Convention Centre Kuching to attend another function.
During the brief visit, Anwar conveyed his condolences on behalf of the government to Nancy and her family.
"I came to offer my condolences and pray that she remains strong in facing this test," he said.
Kamil, 65, died at Hospital Ampang in Kuala Lumpur at 6.38pm last Thursday.
He was laid to rest at the Samariang Muslim Cemetery in Petra Jaya at 5.15pm on Friday.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Al Jazeera rejects Israeli accusations against journalist killed in Gaza
Article:
DOHA: Al Jazeera today rejected Israeli accusations that one of its journalists, killed in Gaza a day earlier, was a Hamas operative, as family and colleagues mourned the cameraman in the Palestinian territory.
The Qatar-based network said in a statement that it "condemns the Israeli occupation army's baseless accusations, which seek to justify its crimes against Al Jazeera journalists and cameramen in Gaza, most recently the killing of cameraman Ahmed Wishah."
The Israeli military said in a statement issued late on Saturday that Wishah was killed in a "precise strike" alongside two other Hamas militants and that he had served as a "sniper operative" in Hamas.
"Alongside his work as an Al Jazeera photojournalist in recent years, Wishah was an operative in Hamas' military wing," the military said.
The Israeli military provided no evidence to support the accusations.
AFP footage captured in Deir el-Balah in central Gaza showed friends and family grieving over Wishah's lifeless body in the area's Al-Aqsa Hospital.
The cameraman could be seen laid out on a steel tray in the hospital's morgue in a cabinet with other bodies.
Bilal Abu Samak, a freelance journalist in Gaza said the killing had left him "in shock."
"For the first time, I found myself unable to film. The occupation deliberately kills journalists and anyone covering events on the ground," Samak added.
In its Sunday statement, Al Jazeera said an "Israeli campaign of incitement has relentlessly spread false allegations and baseless accusations against Al Jazeera staff."
The network called the effort a "smear campaign" and "a transparent and futile attempt to justify the deliberate targeting of journalists and cameramen."
"These attempts deceive no one," the network added.
On Saturday, Al Jazeera reported that Wishah was killed in an Israeli airstrike that targeted a house in the Bureij refugee camp.
Ahmed Wishah's brother Mohammed Wishah, also an Al Jazeera journalist, was killed by Israeli shelling in April, the Qatari broadcaster said at the time.
According to the media rights group Reporters Without Borders, Israeli forces have killed more than 220 journalists since the war in Gaza erupted. At least 70 of them were killed in the context of their professional duties.
The Israeli military has said it does not deliberately target journalists. - AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Woman, accomplice remanded over alleged staged kidnapping for ransom
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Two individuals, including a woman believed to have staged her own kidnapping to obtain RM18,000 in ransom money, have been remanded for 14 days beginning today to assist in investigations.
Bukit Aman Criminal Investigation Department director Datuk M. Kumar said the remand order was obtained from the Johor Bahru Magistrate's Court today.
"The two individuals will be brought to Sarawak to facilitate the completion of investigations, and the case is being investigated under Section 3(1) of the Kidnapping Act 1961 for kidnapping for ransom," he said when contacted today.
In a statement yesterday, Kumar said the woman, who is in her 20s, was arrested after being found safe during an Ops Scorpion raid at a house in Taman Perniagaan Setia, Johor Bahru, at about 3.30pm.
He said the operation was launched following a report lodged by the woman's 43-year-old mother at the Padawan district police headquarters in Sarawak on Friday, claiming that
her daughter had been abducted
by unknown individuals.
During the raid, police also arrested a 27-year-old man with three previous records involving drug-related and criminal offences, and seized four mobile phones.
However, Kumar said preliminary investigations revealed inconsistencies in the alleged victim's account of the kidnapping, leading to her arrest to assist in investigations. — BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Traveller scheme for Malaysia-Singapore workers in final stage, says Ramanan
Article:
JOHOR BARU: The proposal paper for the Traveller scheme, which provides social protection for Malaysians working abroad, particularly in Singapore, is in its final stages ahead of implementation.
Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan said the scheme, expected to benefit about 480,000 Malaysians commuting daily to Singapore, would be tabled in the upcoming parliamentary session starting tomorrow.
He said the ministry and the Social Security Organisation (Perkeso) had completed the proposal paper and were finalising approvals, expected by August before enforcement.
"The parliamentary session begins tomorrow, so we will start tabling the proposal paper. We will also hold engagement sessions with MPs to explain the programme's benefits," he said.
"Once approved by the Dewan Rakyat and Dewan Negara, the scheme will be implemented as soon as possible for Johoreans commuting between the state and Singapore," he said after launching the Karnival Lindung Kerjaya Madani at the Setia Tropika Convention Centre here today.
Ramanan said the
scheme
would be implemented by extending coverage under Act 789 or the Self-Employment Social Security Scheme under Perkeso, allowing eligible individuals to contribute and access eight types of benefits.
Meanwhile, he said Johor's unemployment rate remained low at 2.4 per cent in the first quarter of this year, below the national average of 2.9 per cent.
He said the state's labour force participation rate stood at 71.6 per cent, also above the national average of 70.9 per cent.
"This positive performance was driven by job-matching efforts through the MyFutureJobs portal, alongside cooperation between the government, employers and relevant agencies," he said.
He added that 66,661 job vacancies were advertised through MyFutureJobs in the first six months of the year, while 361,067 job seekers registered in Johor.
Of those, 161,893 people were successfully placed across various sectors.
The Karnival Lindung Kerjaya Madani brought together 20 employers offering more than 2,000 job opportunities, including high-skilled positions with salaries of up to RM16,000 a month.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Fahmi calls for greater recognition, support for local music industry
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The local music industry should be recognised and supported for its important contribution to bringing people together and helping shape Malaysia's cultural identity, said Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil.
He said music has an extraordinary ability to transcend barriers of language, culture and background, resonating with people across all segments of society.
Fahmi also paid tribute to those in the creative industry for their enduring contributions to advancing the nation's music industry.
"Each creation embodies a story, a soul and a voice that add colour and character to society.
"Thank you to all artistes, musicians, composers, singers, producers and everyone in the industry who continue to enrich and sustain the local music scene," he said in a Facebook post marking World Music Day today.
He also expressed hope that the local music industry would continue to flourish and receive the appreciation it deserves.
– Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: From Gus Fring to Shahada: Breaking Bad star Giancarlo Esposito embraces Islam
Article:
ANKARA: American actor Giancarlo Esposito, best known for his role as Gus Fring in the television series Breaking Bad, has embraced Islam during a stay in Saudi Arabia, Anadolu Ajansi reported, citing local media.
Saudi Gazette reported that General Entertainment Authority chairman Turki Al-Sheikh said Esposito recited the Shahada, the Islamic declaration of faith, and joined members of a production team in prayer at a mosque while filming in the kingdom.
According to Al-Sheikh, the actor's decision followed positive experiences during his time in Saudi Arabia and interactions with Muslims while working on a production there.
He said Esposito also expressed appreciation for the community and hospitality he encountered during filming.
A video shared on social media showed the actor praying alongside members of the production team inside a mosque, the report said.
Esposito has built a decades-long career in film and television and is also known for his performances in Better Call Saul, The Mandalorian and other productions.
The report said he gained international recognition and received multiple award nominations for his portrayal of Gus Fring.
According to Saudi Gazette, Esposito is currently involved in filming Seven Dogs, one of several major productions being developed as Saudi Arabia expands its entertainment and film sectors and seeks to attract international productions and talent.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sabah ramps up enforcement of amended fisheries act
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: The Sabah Fisheries Department will strengthen manpower, technology and cooperation to ensure effective enforcement of the amended fisheries law in state waters.
Its director Datin Dr Shuhadah Mustapha said enforcement capacity would be improved through training, monitoring systems such as the Integrated Vessel Monitoring System, and cooperation with national security agencies.
She said enforcement would involve joint operations between the Fisheries Department, the Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency, police and navy, with emphasis on monitoring the Exclusive Economic Zone and protecting sensitive marine areas.
Shuhadah was commenting on the Fisheries Act 1985 (Amendment) 2025, which came into force on June 15.
She said the amendments expanded enforcement powers, allowing inspections, detentions and seizures to be carried out more effectively.
The amendments aim to conserve fish stocks and marine biodiversity, curb illegal fishing, increase penalties and strengthen deterrence through improved monitoring technology.
She said penalties for offences involving foreign vessel encroachment and licence breaches had been increased.
"The enforcement of Act A1780 reflects the government's commitment to safeguarding fisheries resources and ensuring a continuous food supply," she said.
She added that new provisions include a ban on fishing in areas affected by Harmful Algal Bloom incidents or chemical pollution to ensure seafood safety.
She said successful implementation required cooperation from fishing communities, operators and enforcement agencies.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Older Persons Bill crucial amid ageing nation challenges, says Lee Lam Thye
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The proposed Older Persons Bill is timely and essential, as Malaysia grapples with the challenges of an ageing population, said Senior Citizens Advisory Council member Tan Sri Lee Lam Thye.
He said the number of senior citizens in the country is rising rapidly, alongside longer life expectancy.
He said greater responsibility is needed to ensure that the elderly population is protected, respected and able to live with dignity, safety and proper care.
"The bill is important as it will provide a more comprehensive legal and policy framework to protect the rights and welfare of senior citizens.
"Many senior citizens today face various challenges, including neglect, financial problems, loneliness, poor access to healthcare, abuse and social isolation.
"Some are left without proper family support, while others struggle to meet daily needs due to rising living costs and inadequate protection systems," he said in a statement here today.
He said the proposed law should address key areas such as healthcare access, social protection, prevention of elder abuse, mental health support, affordable housing, accessible public facilities, employment opportunities for active seniors and long-term care services.
"It should also strengthen mechanisms to protect senior citizens from physical, emotional and financial abuse.
"Senior citizens should also be recognised as valuable members of the nation who contribute significantly to national development. Their wisdom, experience and sacrifices should be appreciated, protected and respected," he said.
He said the bill should promote stronger family and community responsibility in caring for the elderly, while encouraging active ageing and greater participation of seniors in society.
He said Malaysia must make comprehensive preparations for the upcoming demographic shift to avoid a major social and healthcare crisis in the future.
"We hope the government will ensure that the law enacted is not merely symbolic but truly effective in improving the quality of life, dignity, independence and well-being of senior citizens nationwide.
"A caring nation is ultimately judged by how it treats its elderly. Therefore, protecting the dignity and welfare of senior citizens must remain a national priority," he said.
Women, Family and Community Development Minister Datuk Seri Nancy Shukri said the proposed bill seeks to prioritise family reconciliation, while also establishing a
legal framework to support government initiatives on elderly care
.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Viral data leak claims old incident, unrelated to current platforms - NSC
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The National Security Council (NSC) has clarified that claims of a personal data leak circulating on social media refer to information believed to have originated from cybersecurity incidents reported before 2022 and are not linked to any current platform.
The council through the National Cyber Security Agency (NACSA), said the information was believed to have been unlawfully obtained through cyber intrusions targeting various systems prior to 2022 and is now being redistributed through online platforms without authorisation.
"NACSA emphasises that providing, disseminating or granting access to information obtained unlawfully constitutes an offence under Malaysian law, even if the service is hosted outside the country," the council said in a statement said.
The NACSA, together with MyNIC and the Personal Data Protection Department, has taken immediate action, including engaging foreign service providers to remove and block access to the websites concerned, it added.
NACSA is working closely with the police to conduct digital forensic investigations aimed at identifying those involved and bringing them to justice.
The council also advised Malaysians not to use or obtain services that offer access to unlawfully acquired information, as doing so contributes to the spread of cybercrime and violates Malaysian law.
"This incident further underscores the urgent need to strengthen national legislation. The Cyber Crime Bill, which will be tabled in Parliament, introduces more comprehensive provisions and stricter penalties for various forms of cybercrime, including system intrusions and data theft," NSC said.
The proposed provisions in the bill include the criminalisation of unauthorised access to, or damage of, computer systems and programmes without lawful authority or legitimate purpose, and defining identity theft involving the unauthorised use of another person's identity with the intent to commit a crime as an offence.
The council said that the Cyber Security Act 2024, which came into force in August 2024, requires National Critical Information Infrastructure (NCII) entities to implement comprehensive protection measures, including compliance with codes of practice, risk assessments and periodic security audits to enhance the country's cyber resilience.
It also pointed out that MyDigital ID, with more than 16 million registrations, is not a personal data storage system but functions as an identity verification platform that authenticates users directly with the National Registration Department, helping to ensure the authenticity of user identities and making digital transactions more secure.
"The widespread adoption of MyDigital ID across government and private-sector applications, including telecommunications and banking services, will further strengthen digital transaction security and help prevent identity theft," the council said.
It reiterated the government's priority to ensure that the benefits of digital transformation can be enjoyed safely by all Malaysians through a cybersecurity-focused approach, and that the NACSA and the council were ready to address any potential cybersecurity threats. —  BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Five remanded after missing teenage cousins found safe in Machang
Article:
KOTA BARU: Police have arrested five men in connection with the disappearance of two teenage cousins from Kampung Baloh near Kok Lanas, who were found safe in Machang yesterday after being reported missing since Thursday night.
Machang police chief Superintendent Azuhar Md Nor said the suspects were detained in Machang yesterday evening.
He said police obtained remand orders against all five men at the Machang magistrate's court this morning to facilitate investigations.
"Two of the suspects have been remanded for five days, while the remaining three, who are friends of the two main suspects, have been remanded for two days," he said when contacted today.
The two girls, Nor Syahzanani Mohd Sulaiman, 15, and Nur Kaseh Nalisya Mohd Ramzul Nazrie, 16,
were found at a homestay
in Machang yesterday, about 25km from their homes.
Their families lodged separate police reports on Friday
after the teenagers failed to return home
following an outing in Kota Baru on Thursday evening.
Following their discovery, both girls were taken to Kuala Krai Hospital for medical examination and observation.
Police are continuing investigations to determine the circumstances surrounding the incident.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor polls: Stop dragging royal institution into politics, says Ramanan
Article:
JOHOR BARU: PKR vice-president Datuk Seri R. Ramanan has criticised political leaders for dragging the royal institution into political manoeuvring ahead of the 16th Johor election.
He said political competition must be conducted with integrity and should not involve using other parties to fight political battles on behalf of any leader.
"When we want to fight, we must fight with integrity. If you want to fight, you must go down to the ground and fight, not ask others to fight for you," he said after launching the Karnival Lindung Kerjaya Madani at the Setia Tropika Convention Centre here today.
"You must fight your own battles. That is a leader with integrity. If you cannot fight, ask your president to come down and fight. Do not ask others to fight for you."
He added that it was wrong to involve the royal institution in political attacks.
"I am very disappointed when the royal institution is used. It is not right," he said.
Ramanan, who is also Sungai Buloh member of parliament, said he was saddened to see the Regent of Johor being criticised on social media following recent political developments.
"It is very wrong. I am disappointed and very sad to see the Regent of Johor being attacked on social media. This matter has nothing to do with the royal institution," he said.
Yesterday, Tunku Mahkota Ismail Sultan Ibrahim, Regent of Johor, attended a podcast session in Muar alongside caretaker Johor menteri besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi.
The Regent spoke on several issues involving the federal and state governments, including the return of tax revenue to the state.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia's automotive sector in high gear despite May setback
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's automotive sector is expected to stay resilient in 2026, with total industry volume (TIV) forecast to ease slightly to 780,000 units as national brands continue to dominate the market.
Hong Leong Investment Bank Bhd (HLIB) said May TIV fell 15.1 per cent month-on-month and 12.1 per cent year-on-year, mainly due to Perodua's temporary plant maintenance shutdown.
Despite the monthly decline, cumulative TIV for the first five months of 2026 remained largely stable at 315,600 units, supported by strong sales from Proton, Mazda and Jaecoo.
Electric vehicle (EV) sales reached 5,000 units in May, accounting for 8.2 per cent of total industry volume, with Proton and BYD emerging as key growth drivers.
HLIB expects national brands to maintain their momentum throughout 2026, while non-national brands face increasing pressure from tougher competition on pricing, refreshed models and enhanced product offerings.
"We expect national OEMs (original equipment manufacturers) to maintain their sales momentum in 2026, while non-nationals may continue to face pressure amid intensifying competition in terms of pricing, refreshed designs, and enhanced product features," it said.
Following the latest earnings season, HLIB upgraded its automotive sector view to "Overweight" from "Neutral", citing improved prospects across key industry players.
The firm raised its recommendations on Bermaz Auto Bhd and DRB-HICOM Bhd to "Buy" from "Hold".
"Our top picks for the sector remain MBM Resources Bhd and Sime Darby Bhd, given their leverage onto Perodua's sustaining sales volume," HLIB said.
It added that Sime Darby is also expected to benefit from continued strength in its Industrial segment and a recovery in its China motor division.
Both MBM Resources and Sime Darby are also supported by attractive dividend yields of five to six per cent, HLIB said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Africa, Asia increase food imports from Russia amid growing el-Nino concerns
Article:
MOSCOW: Countries across Africa, parts of Asia, particularly West Asia, are increasing imports of Russian food products amid uncertainty surrounding the Strait of Hormuz and concerns over the approaching El Nino climate phenomenon, according to Russia's state-owned Rosselkhozbank (RSHB) on Sunday, reported Sputnik/RIA Novosti.
El Nino is a characterised by unusually warm sea surface temperatures in the equatorial Pacific Ocean. The resulting temperature shifts can trigger major weather disruptions worldwide, causing droughts in some regions and flooding in others.
"During the first four months of 2026, Russian agricultural exports increased by 4.9 million tonnes, or 21.8 per cent, compared with the same period last year. In particular, amid instability in the Strait of Hormuz and anticipated El Nino-related climate fluctuations, many countries in Africa, parts of Asia, particularly West Asia are building up agricultural stockpiles due to concerns over potential fertiliser shortages and droughts," the bank said.
According to RSHB, Russian food exports to China rose by more than one-third in January–April 2026 compared with the same period a year earlier.
Egypt increased imports of Russian sunflower oil by more than 25 per cent and dried chickpeas by 74 per cent, while Algeria boosted purchases of Russian soybean oil by more than a quarter, RSHB said.
"Russian food exporters benefit from several major competitive advantages, including stable production volumes, established logistics routes, friendly relations with importers in Asia and Africa, and, of course, high product quality. This facilitates their expansion, including into premium segments of high-margin markets such as China and the Arab world," RSHB's Centre for Industry Expertise said.
Forecasts published by the European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts in May said that Pacific Ocean temperatures could rise to more than three degrees Celsius above normal by September–October, potentially marking the second-highest El Nino temperature peak on record. – Bernama-Sputnik/RIA Novosti
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: A-GC, MACC urged to publish reasons for corruption compounds
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Attorney-General's Chambers (A-GC) and the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) should publish summaries explaining decisions to compound corruption-related offences, particularly in high-profile cases, says an anti-graft watchdog.
Malaysia Corruption Watch (MCW) said the move would help strengthen public confidence in the justice system.
"In high-profile cases, the A-GC and MACC should consider publishing a summary of reasons for compound decisions without affecting the confidentiality of investigations," said MCW president Jais Abdul Karim.
"This is important to avoid perceptions that influential individuals are treated differently from ordinary members of the public," he told the New Straits Times.
He was responding to the
A-GC's explanation
that compounds issued in corruption-linked cases were not a "free pass", but were subject to strict legal requirements, scrutiny and considerations of public interest.
Previously, Jais had raised concerns that the use of compounds and settlements in corruption cases could create the perception that such offences wereresolved without trial.
He said the A-GC's latest statement had helped clarify that compounds were not an easy route for those implicated in corruption.
"However, the key issue is not merely whether such powers exist under the law, but how they are exercised and whether they maintain public confidence in the justice system," he said.
Jais welcomed the A-GC's assurance that decisions were made following thorough assessment, additional investigations where necessary, and consideration of public interest.
He said the principle that "justice must not only be done, but must also be seen to be done" should guide all decisions involving compounds or withdrawal of charges.
While the law allows settlements under Sections 92(1) and 92(4) of the Anti-Money Laundering, Anti-Terrorism Financing and Proceeds of Unlawful Activities Act, he said their use must remain exceptional and not the norm.
Jais acknowledged that in certain circumstances – including insufficient evidence, the death of key witnesses or difficulties in obtaining international legal assistance – a trial may not be practical.
"In such situations, the immediate recovery of assets through compounds may better serve the public interest," he added.
On June 3, a former Natural Resources, Environment and Climate Change Ministry officer was
freed
of a bribery charge involving a gold bracelet after paying a compound to the MACC.
Sessions Court judge Suzana Hussin discharged Nur Fazreen Kamal, 45, after the prosecution informed the court that the accused had settled the compound offered on May 7.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Territorial Army vital in strengthening national security, says Raja Muda of Perlis
Article:
ARAU: The Raja Muda of Perlis, Tuanku Syed Faizuddin Putra Jamalullail, said the Territorial Army will continue to uphold its role under the concept of "To Raise, Train and Maintain" personnel.
His Royal Highness, who is also commander of the Territorial Army's 504 Regiment, said that "raise" in the context of mobilisation is not merely about increasing numbers, but ensuring the right human resources are ready to be absorbed, while "train" requires quality training that enables them to operate in integration with the regular armed forces, Berita Harian reported.
"Meanwhile, the concept of 'maintain' ensures their readiness is consistently preserved for augmentation purposes at any time," His Royal Highness said when officiating the closing parade ceremony of the Basic Volunteer Soldier Course for the army.
Tuanku Syed Faizuddin Putra's daughter, Sharifah Farah Adriana, was among those who completed the basic course, which was held from June 7 to 20 at Universiti Malaysia Perlis (UniMAP).
His Highness said Sharifah Farah Adriana's completion of the course clearly demonstrates that national defence is a shared responsibility.
"The involvement of the royal institution reinforces the message that the spirit of love for the nation must be nurtured across all levels of society."
The Raja Muda of Perlis said the effort is a key element in strengthening the army in facing any security challenges.
"In efforts to strengthen the Territorial Army, promotional activities will continue to be intensified and closely monitored to attract more participation from physically fit young people who meet the minimum requirements to join," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pickleball row sparks calls for stricter council control on court approvals
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A viral dispute over a late-night pickleball game in Petaling Jaya has sparked wider debate over whether local councils should play a greater role in approving court locations and setting operating hours near residential areas.
The incident, which allegedly escalated from a noise complaint into a confrontation, has
drawn attention to the rapid spread of pickleball facilities
in urban neighbourhoods and concerns over noise control and enforcement.
While social media users largely condemned the altercation, many said the issue stemmed from planning and regulation rather than individual players.
Some netizens argued that city councils should take greater responsibility in approving court locations, building specifications and operating hours.
"City councils need to play a part in approving court location, the building structure establishment and operating hours," said commenter Willie Jee.
Others said operators should not be solely responsible for setting hours, particularly when facilities are located near residential areas.
Several said that players who booked late-night sessions were using facilities operating within approved hours and should not be blamed.
Patrick Leong said criticism should be directed at policymakers and operators rather than players, adding that complaints should be channelled through proper avenues such as local councils.
Others highlighted the need for improved urban planning as pickleball gains popularity nationwide.
Dinesh Jegarajan suggested indoor facilities with noise mitigation measures would be more suitable in residential areas, adding that open-air courts should face stricter cut-off times, such as 10pm.
The debate also extended to enforcement, with commenters urging residents to continue lodging complaints through official channels.
The incident has reignited discussion on how local authorities balance commercial recreation, community well-being and residential quality of life.
As pickleball courts proliferate in densely populated areas, calls are growing for councils to review approvals, tighten noise controls and reassess operating hours near homes.
The controversy has raised broader questions over whether clearer guidelines are needed for emerging sports with different noise impacts from traditional court games.
The issue has evolved into a wider conversation on planning, enforcement and the role of local authorities in managing competing urban needs.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Missing cousins found safe after two-day search, five arrested
Article:
KOTA BARU: Two teenage cousins who had been missing since Thursday night were found safe in Machang yesterday evening, about 32 hours after their families lodged separate police reports on Friday morning.
Nor Syahzanani Mohd Sulaiman, 15, and Nur Kaseh Nalisya Mohd Ramzul Nazrie, 16, were found at a homestay in Machang, about 25km from their homes in Kampung Baloh near Kok Lanas.
Police have since arrested five suspects to assist in investigations into the girls' disappearance.
Speaking to NST, their aunt, Fatihah Zakaria, said the teenagers are currently under observation at Kuala Krai Hospital.
"The families are relieved that they have finally been found and are grateful that Allah has answered our prayers.
"We would like to thank the police for their efforts in locating the girls, as well as our relatives and members of the public for their prayers and support throughout the ordeal.
"The two are in good health, but they are still a little traumatised by what happened.
"The families need some time to be with the girls, so please give us some space," she said today.
The 43-year-old housewife said the past two days had been an emotional ordeal for the families, who spent countless hours searching for the girls and appealing to the public for information on their whereabouts.
It was reported yesterday that the cousins from Kampung Baloh near Kok Lanas
had gone missing after leaving their homes
for an outing in Kota Baru on Thursday evening.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police tracking man over Taman Puchong Prima house trespass
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Police are looking for a man, caught on a closed-circuit television (CCTV), trespassing into a house in Taman Puchong Prima, Subang Jaya.
Subang Jaya police chief Assistant Commissioner Wan Azlan Wan Mamat said police received a report on the incident at 3pm yesterday.
He said the complainant informed that he received a call from his younger sister, who saw a man wearing a white shirt and light blue denim trousers at the back of the house via CCTV footage.
"Initial investigations and review of the CCTV footage found that an unknown man had trespassed into the back area of the complainant's house.
"However, further checks found no loss or damage to property at the premises," he said in a statement today.
He said investigations are ongoing to trace and identify the suspect.
He added that the case is being investigated under Section 448 of the Penal Code for trespassing.
"Police welcome cooperation from those who have information related to this incident or any suspicious activity to contact the Subang Jaya district police headquarters at 03-7862 7222 to assist investigations.
"All information and the identity of informants will be kept confidential," he said.
Earlier, CCTV footage went viral on social media showing a man believed to have trespassed into the back area of a house before leaving the location, while the residence's security alarm system was also triggered.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia well-positioned to lead in energy transition financing: TNB chief new energy officer
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Overwhelming response to two Islamic bond issuances by Tenaga Nasional Bhd (TNB) totalling RM2.5 billion for energy transition projects shows that Malaysia is well-positioned to lead in energy transition financing within the region.
These two sukuk issuances in 2026 which comprised RM1 billion for solar in May and RM1.5 billion for hydro in March, were both oversubscribed by five times, TNB's chief new energy officer Mohd Zarihi Mohd Hashim said.
He said TNB's recent experience reflects this trend, alluding to the massive investor demand gaining momentum regionally as well as globally.
"This outcome indicates strong investor appetite for well-prepared, bankable renewable energy assets backed by clear structures and robust counterparties," he said.
Mohd Zarihi was sharing his perspective at the Energy Transition Conference 2026 (ETCon26) held earlier this month on how Malaysia could translate available funding into well-structured projects that support national net zero ambitions.
He said Malaysia's National Energy Transition Roadmap (NETR) estimates a financing requirement of RM1.2 trillion to RM1.3 trillion over the next 25 years, or approximately RM50 billion annually.
Globally, US$2.3 trillion was deployed towards energy transition projects in 2025 alone, with these figures suggesting that capital availability is not the primary constraint.
Mohd Zarihi said the panel discussion at ETCon26 noted that the more pressing challenge lies in the speed and scale of project deployment.
Projects require stronger preparation, clearer contractual arrangements and more stable policy signals in order to unlock capital efficiently.
Risk-sharing mechanisms, including blended finance, are expected to play an important role, particularly for smaller developers and emerging technologies, he said.
The overwhelming response to TNB's two sukuk issuances is clearly indicative of massive investor demand.
The robust appetite reflects strong institutional confidence in the power utility's energy transition initiatives and the NETR's target to reach net-zero emissions by 2050.
Energy transition financing would include projects for renewable energy such as solar and hydro, green hydrogen, carbon capture, energy efficiency, advancing electric mobility and energy-related initiatives.
Mohd Zarihi said that financing for energy transition projects is expanding rapidly, placing greater emphasis on whether projects are bankable and ready to receive capital.
Elaborating on Malaysia being ready to lead in energy transition financing within the region, he said regulatory frameworks and climate-related guidelines are in place, and capital markets have demonstrated support for transition-focused instruments.
"The priority now is to accelerate the translation of capital into bankable projects, resilient infrastructure and scalable deployment that support the country's transition ambitions," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Hassan: Federal allocations to Johor governed by Constitution
Article:
JOHOR BARU: The Madani Government is guided by the law and the Federal Constitution in matters involving financial allocations to states, including Johor, said Pasir Gudang member of parliament Hassan Abdul Karim.
In a statement, the lawyer said Article 109(1) of the Federal Constitution provides that the Federal Government shall, for every financial year, grant each state a capitation allocation in accordance with Part I of the Tenth Schedule.
He said Article 110(1) further stipulates that states are entitled to revenue derived from taxes, fees and other sources of income as set out in Part III of the Tenth Schedule.
"If any state government is dissatisfied, the matter can be raised through the National Finance Council, which the Federal Government is required to consult on allocations and grants to the states under Article 108(4) of the Federal Constitution," said the lawyer.
As such, Hassan said any Johor government formed after the Johor election on July 11 could refer issues concerning federal allocations to the National Finance Council in accordance with existing legal provisions.
During a session with the people of Johor yesterday, the Regent of Johor, Tunku Ismail, called on the Federal Government to return a more reasonable share of the revenue collected from the state to enable development and welfare programmes to be implemented more effectively.
His Royal Highness said Johor contributes more than RM40 billion annually to the Federal Government, yet only about RM2 billion to RM3 billion is returned to meet the needs of the state's nearly five million residents.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Local shrimp supply unaffected by Thai import ban - Fisheries Dept
Article:
MARANG: Malaysia's shrimp supply is guaranteed to be sufficient even after the country imposed a temporary ban on five shrimp species from Thailand starting June 1.
Fisheries Department director-general Datuk Adnan Hussain said shrimp associations and local farmers have drawn up plans to raise production levels to cater for domestic consumption and overseas markets.
"Our shrimp are also exported to countries including the Gulf states, South Korea, the European Union and Australia. Our supply remains sufficient, and we have received feedback from farmers and associations that they support this temporary restriction and are now planning to increase production capacity to meet domestic and export demand," he told reporters after launching the reopening of the Training Vessel KL Cermin at the Fisheries Academy Malaysia here.
He said Malaysia records annual shrimp imports of about 11,000 metric tonnes from Thailand and exports roughly 3,000 metric tonnes in return.
Adnan said the temporary import ban would protect the domestic industry from an oversupply of imported products and tighten biosecurity controls to ensure the fisheries sector remains free from disease and illegal chemicals.
On May 16, Malaysia strengthened import controls on Thai fishery products by enforcing a Certificate of Analysis (CoA) requirement for siakap (seabass) and imposing a temporary ban on five shrimp species, namely Penaeus esculentes (brown tiger prawn), Fenneropenaeus merguiensis (banana prawn), Penaeus vannamei (whiteleg shrimp), Penaeus monodon (giant tiger prawn) and Penaeus stylirostris (blue shrimp).
Thailand has reportedly indicated it could escalate the matter to the World Trade Organisation and Asean if talks fail to resolve
Malaysia's temporary restriction on imports of the shrimp species
.
Earlier, Adnan said the KL Cermin, which belongs to the Fisheries Academy Malaysia, has returned to service following repairs and upgrades carried out since 2023 at a cost of RM10.14 million, including engine improvements, repairs to the technical trawl system, and the purchase of new fishing gear.
He said the vessel, a modern training ship with a gross capacity of 160 metric tonnes, was specially built to support practical training, research and skills enhancement in the fisheries industry. – Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bersatu reasserts founding role in PN amid Pas row
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bersatu has reminded Pas of the history behind the formation of Perikatan Nasional (PN), insisting the coalition originated from an idea by Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin.
The reminder comes amid an emerging dispute between the two parties over their standing in PN, including the use of the coalition's logo ahead of the Johor and Negri Sembilan elections.
Bersatu information chief Datuk Tun Faisal Ismail Aziz said the origins of PN could not be disputed, as the formation process was led by Bersatu from the outset.
He said the idea to establish PN came from Muhyiddin, who is Bersatu president, and not Pas as some had suggested.
"To this day, no Pas leader can deny that the idea to establish Perikatan Nasional came from Tan Sri Muhyiddin," he said in a Facebook post.
Tun Faisal said PN's constitution was drafted by Bersatu, while the coalition's logo was also produced through the party's president's office.
He said that Bersatu managed the PN registration process until its official approval.
He said that the party handled most operational and administrative matters from PN's formation in 2020 through to the 15th General Election (GE15), with other parties only brought in after key documentation was completed.
"It handled almost every aspect related to the formation and registration of PN," he added.
"Other parties were only invited to meetings after most documentation and registration matters had been completed."
Earlier, it was reported that PN would hold an
emergency meeting
at Pas headquarters tomorrow night to discuss membership and related issues, including the use of the coalition's name.
PN secretary-general Datuk Seri Takiyuddin Hassan had said any activity or meeting using the coalition's name could not proceed without the consent of its chairman, Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Govt lowers subsidised diesel price to RM2.10 from next month
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The government will lower the price of subsidised diesel for Malaysians to RM2.10 per litre from July as part of a nationwide subsidy rationalisation programme.
The Finance Ministry, in a statement, said the move would be implemented through a reformed, uniform diesel subsidy targeting system using the MyKad mechanism, in line with the existing Budi Madani RON95 (Budi95) framework.
The ministry said the reform would ensure Malaysians enjoy the same price and purchasing method nationwide, with diesel subsidies channelled directly to citizens via MyKad verification.
Non-citizens and ineligible parties would be required to purchase diesel at unsubsidised prices.
"In the midst of the ongoing Middle East crisis, which continues to pressure global oil supplies, this move is important to ensure government subsidies reach only Malaysians and to reduce leakages and smuggling of subsidised diesel," the ministry said.
It said the wide price gap between subsidised and unsubsidised diesel had created room for leakages and smuggling, including across borders.
Diesel is currently sold at a subsidised retail price of RM2.15 per litre at all petrol stations in Sabah and Sarawak, compared with RM4.37 per litre for unsubsidised diesel in Peninsular Malaysia.
Under the subsidy retargeting reform, the ministry said retail diesel prices in Sabah and Sarawak would be aligned with those in the peninsula, meaning non-citizens would no longer benefit from the subsidy.
It said Sabah and Sarawak residents with diesel vehicles would see the retail price drop from RM2.15 to RM2.10 per litre.
Further details on the implementation of the diesel subsidy retargeting reform will be announced by Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan on June 22.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: King receives heartfelt Father's Day surprise
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, received a special Father's Day gift from Her Majesty Raja Zarith Sofiah, Queen of Malaysia, as well as their children and grandchildren.
"The meaningful gift reflected the love, appreciation and respect of his children and grandchildren towards His Majesty, who remains the pillar of the family.
"The special moment also highlighted the close-knit family bond that continues to form the foundation of happiness and harmony within the royal household," read a post on Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar Facebook page.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Khaled: Malbatt shielded from ground conflict in Lebanon
Article:
KOTA TINGGI: The Malaysian Battalion (Malbatt) 850-13, deployed on a peacekeeping mission in Lebanon, operates from a central base that offers a strategic security advantage as it is located away from the border area.
Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the battalion's positioning reduces the risk of Malaysian forces becoming involved in any ground fighting in the event of unrest along the Lebanon-Israel border.
He said areas closer to the border are instead monitored by peacekeeping forces from other countries, including France and Indonesia.
"We do have an area that we are guarding. Our advantage is that we are not stationed along the Lebanese-Israeli border but are positioned in the middle. Those deployed closer to the border include French and Indonesian forces.
"So we are safe should a ground battle occur," he told reporters after attending a free fuel programme at BHPetrol Felda Air Tawar 2 here today.
However, Khaled said national forces must not be complacent, as the threat has now shifted to aerial attacks.
He said the risk of attacks involving air assets such as drones and fighter jets remains, regardless of where peacekeeping forces are positioned.
On the standard operating procedures (SOPs) in the event of an emergency, Khaled said existing directives require all Malbatt personnel to remain vigilant and seek shelter in bunkers to ensure their safety.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Three Malaysians busted hours after entering Singapore on scam 'mission'
Article:
SINGAPORE: Three Malaysian men were arrested within six hours of entering Singapore after allegedly arriving on instructions from a scam syndicate to collect cash and gold bars from victims and withdraw illicit proceeds from automated teller machines (ATMs).
The suspects, aged 22 to 24, were charged yesterday over their alleged involvement in cross-border scam operations.
Singapore police said officers from the Anti-Scam Command and the Immigration and Checkpoints Authority launched an operation after receiving information on a 23-year-old Malaysian believed to be linked to scam activities.
Investigations led officers to two alleged accomplices who had entered Singapore with him. All three were arrested shortly after arriving in the republic.
Preliminary investigations found the trio had been directed by a Malaysia-based scam syndicate to carry out ATM withdrawals at various locations across Singapore.
Police seized 69 bank cards allegedly used to withdraw about S$200,000 (RM660,000) linked to more than 20 scam cases.
The 23-year-old was charged with assisting others to retain criminal proceeds, an offence punishable by up to 10 years' imprisonment, a fine of up to S$500,000 (RM1.65 million), or both.
His alleged accomplices, aged 22 and 24, were charged with unauthorised access to computer material, which carries a maximum penalty of two years' jail, a fine of up to S$5,000 (RM16,024), or both.
Police highlighted a growing trend of Malaysians travelling to Singapore to facilitate scam syndicates by collecting cash and valuables from victims.
Authorities warned that scam mules could face up to 12 strokes of the cane, in addition to imprisonment and fines.
"The Singapore Police Force takes a serious stance against anyone involved in scams, and perpetrators will be dealt with in accordance with the law," it said.
In a separate islandwide anti-scam operation between June 4 and 17, police investigated 216 people suspected of involvement in more than 406 scam cases, with reported losses exceeding S$9 million (RM29.7 million).
The cases involved e-commerce, job, investment, government official impersonation, friend impersonation and rental scams.
Singapore recently tightened its anti-scam laws, introducing mandatory caning for scammers and syndicate recruiters. Scam mules involved in laundering criminal proceeds, supplying SIM cards or providing Singpass credentials may also face corporal punishment.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PN to discuss Bersatu's position at key leadership meeting tomorrow
Article:
TEMERLOH: The Perikatan Nasional (PN) leadership council will discuss Bersatu's position in the coalition at a meeting tomorrow, says Pas vice-president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar.
Samsuri, who is also PN chairman, said Bersatu would ultimately decide whether it wished to remain in or leave the opposition coalition.
He said the matter was among the key agenda items amid ongoing speculation over Bersatu's future in PN.
"One of the important agenda items is membership in PN," he said after attending the 40th Kuala Krau Pas annual general meeting at Kompleks Al-Makmur in Kerdau today.
Samsuri said PN's composition remained unchanged, comprising Pas, Bersatu, the Malaysian Indian People's Party (MIPP) and Gerakan.
Any issue involving the membership or status of a component party, he said, must be discussed and decided collectively by the coalition's leadership.
He said a final decision on Bersatu's position would only be known after tomorrow night's meeting, but declined to elaborate on the expected discussions.
The issue resurfaced following speculation over Bersatu's direction amid recent political developments and challenges faced by its leadership.
On June 8, Ps paresident Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang said his party's political relationship with Bersatu had
ended
and left it to the party led by Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin to decide whether it wished to remain in PN.
However, on June 16, Muhyiddin announced that Bersatu had decided to
stay
in the coalition and would contest the Johor and Negri Sembilan state elections under the PN banner.
Tomorrow's meeting is also expected to focus on strengthening the coalition and preparations to address current political developments.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pakistani premier arrives in Switzerland for US-Iran talks
Article:
KARACHI: Pakistan's Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif arrived in Switzerland on Sunday morning to attend technical-level talks between the United States and Iran to end the armed conflict in the Middle East, Anadolu Ajansi (AA) reported, citing local media.
Sharif, along with army chief Field Marshal Asim Munir, will represent Pakistan in the talks on the implementation of the Islamabad Memorandum of Understanding, at Burgenstock, a statement from Pakistan's Foreign Ministry said.
The high-level delegations from Iran, Qatar, and the US will be part of these talks, which mark the
first formal engagement
between the warring sides after the signing of the Islamabad Memorandum of Understanding.
"Pakistan will continue to support and advance the implementation of the understandings reached between the Islamic Republic of Iran and the United States," the statement added.
On the sidelines of the talks, Sharif is also expected to hold bilateral interactions with the participating delegations from Iran, Qatar, Switzerland, and the US to reaffirm Pakistan's "enduring commitment to dialogue and durable peace in the region".
The signing of the deal by US President Donald Trump and Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian had set the stage for direct talks in Switzerland.
The US and Iran had been set to hold the talks in Switzerland on Friday to start a 60-day negotiation period, before the talks were postponed.
After securing a ceasefire on April 8, Pakistan hosted the highest-level direct talks between Washington and Teheran on April 12-13 since they severed diplomatic ties in 1979. — BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: KOPMEDIA sets RM10mil revenue goal, eyes top 50 ranking
Article:
PORT DICKSON: Koperasi Media Prima Bhd (KOPMEDIA) has set an ambitious revenue target of RM10 million by 2030 as it aims to break into the ranks of the top 50 cooperatives in Malaysia.
Chairman Mohd Fadzil Abdul Ghani said the target is achievable through the cooperative's diversified business portfolio and strong support from Media Prima Bhd employees.
Speaking at the cooperative's 58th Annual General Meeting (AGM) at Sri Pena Training Centre here today, he said various sectors, including retail, are already showing positive signs of growth.
"Current initiatives, such as self-service laundries at several higher learning institutions in Melaka and our 'Smart Campus' projects, are progressing well," he said.
He said KOPMEDIA is also venturing into new fields, including human capital development and mobile journalist courses, while continuing to prioritise the welfare of its members.
"We remain committed to improving the socio-economic status of our members through various welfare programmes, including Umrah contributions for eligible members," he said.
The event was launched by the Malaysian Cooperative Commission assistant director of Regulation (FT branch) Mohammad Farhan Mohamad Sabri.
Commenting on the cooperative's performance, Farhan said KOPMEDIA has shown consistent growth for the financial year ending Dec 31, 2025, by exploring new opportunities and providing value-added programmes for its members.
"The success of a cooperative is measured not only by financial performance but also by its ability to improve member welfare.
"KOPMEDIA continues to practise transparent governance while strengthening its business operations," he said.
He said that for the coming year, the cooperative's strategic focus would include expanding its business portfolio, strengthening member financing schemes and widening the 'Jualan Rahmah' (affordable sales) programme.
Other key focus areas include leveraging digitalisation and enhancing human capital development.
Farhan expressed confidence that KOPMEDIA's efficient governance and adherence to the Cooperative Act would eventually place it on par with the country's leading cooperatives.
"Based on their experience and achievements, we believe KOPMEDIA is on the right track. At the regulatory level, we are ready to monitor and assist them in reaching the top tier of cooperatives in Malaysia," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Tekun targets 24-hour loan approvals for small businesses
Article:
MELAKA, June 21 — Tabung Ekonomi Kumpulan Usaha Niaga (Tekun Nasional) aims to approve financing below RM20,000 within 24 hours, said Entrepreneur Development and Cooperatives Minister Steven Sim Chee Keong.
Sim said the quick-approval target helps Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs) secure working capital immediately.
“We are currently running a pilot project (for financing approvals below RM20,000), and we aim to launch it within the next two to three months.
“We want to offer a 24-hour processing period to facilitate MSMEs… so these small businesses do not have to wait long (to secure financing for working capital),” he told reporters after closing Karnival Hebatkan Perniagaan Malaysia (HPM) at Morten Walk here today.
The event was officiated by Melaka Senior Housing, Local Government, Drainage, Climate Change and Disaster Management Committee chairman Datuk Rais Yasin.
Also present were Deputy Entrepreneur Development and Cooperatives Minister Datuk Mohamad Alamin, KUSKOP secretary-general Datuk Seri Khairul Dzaimee Daud, and state Entrepreneur Development, Cooperatives and Consumer Affairs Committee chairman Allex Seah Shoo Chin.
At the event, the ministry also launched the Tekun Nasional Portal, a one-stop digital platform that enables entrepreneurs to obtain financing information, submit online applications, access programme and entrepreneurship training announcements, and locate Tekun Nasional offices nationwide.
Meanwhile, the ministry said in a statement that Tekun Nasional financing applications up to RM100,000 are processed and approved within seven days. SME Bank and Bank Rakyat are also committed to processing and approving financing up to RM1 million within 14 days.
As of May 31, the ministry had approved RM92 million in financing to over 4,300 entrepreneurs in Melaka through its various agencies.
Nationally, as of the same date, the ministry had approved RM5 billion in financing, benefiting more than 180,000 enterprises nationwide.
According to the statement, the ministry targets disbursing RM15 billion in financing to MSMEs nationwide under the PowerUp10k initiative this year.
The three-day HPM carnival, which began on Friday (June 19), ended today. — Bernama

Title: Penang pumps RM129,900 into youth ideas, not just events
Article:
GEORGE TOWN, June 21 — The Penang government has approved RM129,900 under its Youth Development Fund this year to support 68 youth development programmes by 48 associations in the state.
Penang Youth, Sports and Health Committee chairman Daniel Gooi Zi Sen said the funds were part of a total of RM200,000 allocated by the state government under the Youth State Executive Council Meeting.
“The funds are a strategic development to organise 68 programmes encompassing aspects such as skills, marketability, volunteerism and leadership.
“The funds are not mere financial aid but a form of trust given to youth associations to realise ideas, creativity and initiatives through programmes that give real impact to society,” he said in a statement today.
Gooi reminded organisations receiving the fund to implement their programmes with integrity, transparency and efficient management to ensure that every planned initiative achieves its intended objectives.
He said the success of a programme should not be measured solely by the execution of activities, but also by its long-term impact on participants and the wider community. — Bernama

Title: Zambry: Malaysia emerging as global education hub after strong Asia rankings showing
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 21 — The success of Malaysian universities in securing international rankings demonstrates the country’s capability to emerge as a globally respected hub of excellence in education, research and innovation.
Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abd Kadir said that while rankings are not the ultimate goal of a university, they remain an important indicator of the quality, reputation and competitiveness of higher education institutions on the international stage.
According to him, the achievements reflected the collective efforts of lecturers, researchers, students, alumni, staff and industry partners who have consistently worked to elevate the standing of the nation’s higher education sector.
“May this momentum of excellence continue to grow in producing the nation’s best talents and strengthening Malaysia’s position as a preferred destination for higher education,” he said in a Facebook post today.
He also congratulated Universiti Teknologi Petronas (UTP) for becoming the first Malaysian university to secure a place among Asia’s top 40 universities.
Zambry also congratulated the country’s public and private higher education institutions that have continued to strengthen their impressive performance in the latest rankings.
These institutions include Universiti Malaya (UM), Sunway University, Universiti Kebangsaan Malaysia (UKM), Universiti Teknologi Malaysia (UTM), Universiti Sains Malaysia (USM), Universiti Putra Malaysia (UPM), Universiti Utara Malaysia (UUM), Universiti Malaysia Pahang Al-Sultan Abdullah (UMPSA), Universiti Pendidikan Sultan Idris (UPSI) and Universiti Tenaga Nasional (UNITEN).
Media reports stated that UTP climbed to 35th place in the Times Higher Education (THE) Asia University Rankings 2026, up from 43rd position last year.
The report also noted that 27 Malaysian institutions were listed in this year’s rankings, with a record six universities placed among Asia’s top 100 and 11 local universities ranked within the top 200. — Bernama

Title: Bintulu Port shift marks win-win under MA63 framework, says federal minister
Article:
KUCHING, June 21 — The handover of Bintulu Port’s regulatory authority to the Sarawak state government is among the key achievements in the implementation of the Malaysia Agreement 1963 (MA63), said Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Sabah and Sarawak) Datuk Mustapha Sakmud.
He said the move not only recognises Sarawak’s rights as Malaysia’s founding partner but also demonstrates that MA63 can be implemented in a spirit of cooperation, resulting in a win-win situation for both Sarawak and the Federal Government.
“Bintulu Port is no longer just the country’s liquefied natural gas (LNG) export hub; it is evolving into an industrial, logistics and green energy centre that will play an important role in the economic landscape of Malaysia and the Asia-Pacific region.
“Bintulu’s strategic location, coupled with Sarawak’s advantage in renewable energy resources, particularly hydroelectric power, makes it increasingly attractive to investors shifting towards low-carbon operations. This development has the potential to position Sarawak as a key hub for green industries, clean energy and high-value manufacturing in the region,” he said in a statement today.
Mustapha said the implementation of MA63 should not be viewed as a loss of power by the Federal Government, but rather as a strengthening of the Malaysian federation through the recognition of the rights, roles and true potential of Sabah and Sarawak as the country’s founding partners.
The statement was issued following a cheque handover ceremony marking the change in status of Bintulu Port from a federal port to a state port, which took place in Bintulu today.
The ceremony was attended by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim and Sarawak Premier Tan Sri Abang Johari Tun Openg. — Bernama

Title: Viral data‑leak claims stem from pre‑2022 intrusions, unrelated to current systems, says National Security Council
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 21 — The National Security Council (MKN) has clarified that claims of a personal data leak circulating on social media refer to information believed to have originated from cybersecurity incidents reported before 2022 and are not linked to any current platform.
The council through the National Cyber Security Agency (NACSA), said the information was believed to have been unlawfully obtained through cyber intrusions targeting various systems prior to 2022 and is now being redistributed through online platforms without authorisation.
“NACSA emphasises that providing, disseminating or granting access to information obtained unlawfully constitutes an offence under Malaysian law, even if the service is hosted outside the country,” the council said in a statement said.
The NACSA, together with MyNIC and the Personal Data Protection Department, has taken immediate action, including engaging foreign service providers to remove and block access to the websites concerned, it added.
At the same time, NACSA is working closely with the Royal Malaysia Police to conduct digital forensic investigations aimed at identifying those involved and bringing them to justice.
The council also advised Malaysians not to use or obtain services that offer access to unlawfully acquired information, as doing so contributes to the spread of cybercrime and violates Malaysian law.
“This incident further underscores the urgent need to strengthen national legislation. The Cyber Crime Bill, which will be tabled in Parliament, introduces more comprehensive provisions and stricter penalties for various forms of cybercrime, including system intrusions and data theft,” MKN said.
The proposed provisions in the bill include the criminalisation of unauthorised access to, or damage of, computer systems and programmes without lawful authority or legitimate purpose, and defining identity theft involving the unauthorised use of another person’s identity with the intent to commit a crime as an offence.
The council explained that the Cyber Security Act 2024, which came into force in August 2024, requires National Critical Information Infrastructure (NCII) entities to implement comprehensive protection measures, including compliance with codes of practice, risk assessments and periodic security audits to enhance the country’s cyber resilience.
It also pointed out that MyDigital ID, with more than 16 million registrations, is not a personal data storage system but functions as an identity verification platform that authenticates users directly with the National Registration Department, helping to ensure the authenticity of user identities and making digital transactions more secure.
“The widespread adoption of MyDigital ID across government and private-sector applications, including telecommunications and banking services, will further strengthen digital transaction security and help prevent identity theft,” the council said.
It reiterated the government’s priority to ensure that the benefits of digital transformation can be enjoyed safely by all Malaysians through a cybersecurity-focused approach, and that the NACSA and the council were ready to address any potential cybersecurity threats. — Bernama

Title: Old bruises, unanswered questions: Death of 64-year-old at Sungai Petani care home sparks police probe
Article:
SUNGAI PETANI, June 21 — A 64-year-old man’s death at a care centre here has sparked a police investigation after his family alleged he had previously been involved in a fight with another resident.
The case has put the spotlight on conditions at the unnamed facility, with officers now working to piece together what happened in the man’s final days.
Kuala Muda district police chief Assistant Commissioner Hanyan Ramlan said the family lodged a report yesterday and an investigation is now underway,
Harian Metro
reported today.
A preliminary examination of the body revealed several old injuries, though police have yet to establish when or how they were sustained.
“The family claims the victim had a disagreement with another resident at the care centre, but the matter is still under investigation,” Hanyan told the Malay newspaper when contacted.
“Police have recorded statements from relevant individuals to assist in the investigation,” he added.
The body has been sent to hospital for a post-mortem examination, with police now awaiting the forensic report to determine the actual cause of death.
Hanyan urged the public to refrain from speculation, warning that premature conclusions could compromise the probe.
“At this stage, the case has been classified as a sudden death report. However, if investigations reveal elements of criminal wrongdoing, further action will be taken,” he said.

Title: Job losses near 39,000 in five months with Malaysians aged 25 to 39 hardest hit, says HR Ministry
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 21 — Nearly 39,000 Malaysians lost their jobs in the first five months of this year, prompting the government to strengthen support measures for affected workers.
According to data cited by ETHRWorldSEA, the Human Resources Ministry said 38,953 workers were recorded as having lost their jobs between January and May under the Employment Insurance System (EIS).
Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan said most of those affected were aged between 25 and 39.
Men accounted for 23,536 cases, while women made up 15,417 cases.The job losses were mainly recorded in the manufacturing, wholesale and retail trade, motor vehicle repair, and administrative support sectors, largely due to business restructuring and operational adjustments by companies.
Ramanan said the ministry is expanding employment and skills programmes to help affected workers secure new jobs, enhance their skills and transition into industries with stronger demand.
Despite the rise in layoffs, he said Malaysia's labour market remains resilient, with the unemployment rate standing at 3.0 per cent in April and the labour force reaching 17.33 million people. — Daily Express

Title: Legal action likely over Tambirat Waterfront project collapse, delaying Phase Two and the regatta, says Sarawak minister
Article:
KUCHING, June 21 — The stalled Phase One of the Tambirat Waterfront project is likely to end up in court following the collapse of part of the riverside development, a setback that has also delayed the return of the long-awaited Tambirat Regatta, said Datuk Seri Abdul Karim Rahman Hamzah.
The Minister of Youth, Sports and Entrepreneur Development said the regatta had originally been planned to coincide with the completion of the waterfront’s first phase, with Premier Tan Sri Abang Johari Openg expected to attend the event.
“However, if I’m not mistaken, about four months before it was due to be completed, part of the waterfront collapsed.
“As a result, the project had to be halted,” he told reporters after the closing and prize presentation ceremony of the Sarawak Youth Business Idea Competition (SaYBIC) 2026 at the Sarawak Sports Village here.
Abdul Karim said information available to him suggested there may have been negligence on the part of a subcontractor, although the matter could only be conclusively determined through legal proceedings or a thorough investigation.
“We do know that there were some miscalculations or negligence by certain parties in handling the project, which led to the collapse.
“However, I cannot reveal the details because this may become a legal matter, and commenting publicly could affect the parties involved,” he said.
According to Abdul Karim, the waterfront project, initiated before the last state election, comprises two phases.
The collapse of Phase One has also delayed the implementation of Phase Two, which covers a longer stretch of the development.
Despite the setback, he said the state government remains committed to completing the project, adding that the Premier had, in principle, agreed for Phase Two to proceed.
“In my view, Phase One of the project will most likely proceed through legal channels. We want to move ahead with Phase Two, and we are now looking into the matter.
“However, we will be more cautious because we do not want what happened in Phase One to happen again,” he said.
Abdul Karim said the people of Kampung Tambirat have been waiting patiently for the waterfront to be completed, as it would pave the way for various community activities to resume, including the Tambirat Regatta.
“We hope the people of Kampung Tambirat will continue to be patient. Once the waterfront is completed, there are many activities that can be organised there, including the Tambirat Regatta,” he said. — The Borneo Post

Title: Malaysia’s shrimp supply stable as industry backs temporary Thai import ban, says DOF
Article:
MARANG, June 21 — Malaysia’s shrimp supply is guaranteed to be sufficient even after the country imposed a temporary ban on five shrimp species from Thailand starting June 1.
Department of Fisheries (DOF) director-general, Datuk Adnan Hussain, said shrimp associations and local farmers have drawn up plans to raise production levels to cater for domestic consumption and overseas markets.
“Our shrimp are also exported to countries including the Gulf states, South Korea, the European Union and Australia. Our supply remains sufficient, and we have received feedback from farmers and associations that they support this temporary restriction and are now planning to increase production capacity to meet domestic and export demand,” he said.
He spoke to reporters after officiating the reopening of the Training Vessel KL Cermin at the Fisheries Academy Malaysia here.
He said Malaysia records annual shrimp imports of about 11,000 metric tonnes from Thailand and exports roughly 3,000 metric tonnes in return.
Adnan said the temporary import ban would protect the domestic industry from an oversupply of imported products and tighten biosecurity controls to ensure the fisheries sector remains free from disease and illegal chemicals.
On May 16, Malaysia strengthened import controls on Thai fishery products by enforcing a Certificate of Analysis (CoA) requirement for siakap (seabass) and imposing a temporary ban on five shrimp species, namely Penaeus esculentes (brown tiger prawn), Fenneropenaeus merguiensis (banana prawn), Penaeus vannamei (whiteleg shrimp), Penaeus monodon (giant tiger prawn) and Penaeus stylirostris (blue shrimp).
Thailand has reportedly indicated it could escalate the matter to the World Trade Organisation and Asean if talks fail to resolve Malaysia’s temporary restriction on imports of the shrimp species.
Earlier, Adnan said the KL Cermin, which belongs to the Fisheries Academy Malaysia, has returned to service following repairs and upgrades carried out since 2023 at a cost of RM10.14 million, including engine improvements, repairs to the technical trawl system, and the purchase of new fishing gear.
He said the vessel, a modern training ship with a gross capacity of 160 metric tonnes, was specially built to support practical training, research and skills enhancement in the fisheries industry. — Bernama

Title: Police seal Bercham storm‑hit areas, tighten movement controls to curb theft
Article:
IPOH, June 21 — Police have sealed off five zones within disaster-affected areas in Bercham and restricted movement in and out to prevent theft or house break-ins.
Ipoh district police chief ACP Muhammad Najib Hamzah said some flexibility is given to residents who wish to enter or leave the affected areas, particularly those cleaning up their homes at night.
“However, we will be stricter in restricting movement at night as some affected areas, including Anjung Bercham, are still without electricity.
“If residents want to clean their homes or belongings at night, we will monitor and verify whether the houses belong to them, as we are concerned about potential theft disguised as clean-up activities,” he said at a press conference after inspecting the Incident Control Post (PKTK) at Bercham police station here today.
He said as of 8 am, police had received 492 storm-related reports through Op Bencana, adding that there is no deadline for victims to lodge reports.
Meanwhile, Muhammad Najib said the total losses in the affected areas have yet to be determined.
Yesterday, Ipoh Barat Member of Parliament M. Kulasegaran reportedly said the storm, which struck on Friday, was extraordinary and affected more than 200 houses in Bercham.
Kulasegaran, who is also Deputy Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Law and Institutional Reform), said the unprecedented incident is believed to have been caused by a landspout phenomenon.
Among the areas affected are Anjung Bercham Utara, Taman Mujur, Kampung Bercham, Kampung Tersusun Tasek, Taman Pusat Bercham and Taman Indah Sakti. — Bernama

Title: MOF: Diesel price to drop to RM2.10 per litre from July 2026 under MyKad‑based subsidy mechanism
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 21 — The subsidised diesel price for Malaysians will fall to RM2.10 per litre starting July 2026, following an announcement by Prime Minister and Finance Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim in Bintulu today.
According to a press statement issued by the Ministry of Finance and shared on social media by Communications Minister Fahmi Fadzil, the price adjustment is part of the Madani government’s plan to standardise and target diesel subsidies nationwide.
